cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which option is most similar to Georgia O'Keeffe's relationship with nature. Let me read through the passage again and see what it says about her connection with nature. The passage mentions that O'Keeffe was born on a farm in Wisconsin and never lost her bond with the land. She painted the things that were most important to her, which were simplified portrayals of nature. Later, when she visited the Western desert, she was moved by the landscape and started painting its images, including cows' skulls and bones. After her husband's death, she moved to New Mexico and continued using elements of the desert like the horizon lines, flowers, rocks, hills, and sky as subjects. She continued to produce works inspired by the Western desert until her death. The passage also states that her work was more identifiably American in its treatment of natural forms. So, O'Keeffe
